question: What is the exact legal name of your company?
helperText: Provide the full registered name of your company as per the certificate of incorporation or GST registration.
guidanceTips: Enter the exact legal name of your company as it appears on official documents like GST registration, PAN card, or certificate of incorporation.
Avoid using abbreviations, acronyms, or informal names. The name should match your company's official records.
If your company has multiple locations, provide the name of the specific branch or division applying for this insurance policy.

question: What is your business registration number?
helperText: This is the unique identification number assigned to your business by the government. It is typically a combination of letters and numbers.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing Business Registration Number
guidanceTips: The business registration number is typically a 10-15 character alphanumeric code issued by the government, such as your GST registration number or PAN (Permanent Account Number).
Ensure you enter the exact number as it appears on your business registration documents, without any spaces or special characters.
If you are unsure of your business registration number, you can check the documents provided by the government when you registered your business.

question: What is your business's Goods and Services Tax (GST) registration number?
helperText: The GST number is a unique 15-digit identification number assigned to your business by the Government of India. It is mandatory for all businesses in India to have a valid GST registration.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for providing your GST number
guidanceTips: Ensure you enter the exact 15-digit GST number as it appears on your GST registration certificate.
The GST number follows a specific format: XX-AAAAA-NNNN-A-Z-Z-Z, where XX is the state code, AAAAA is the PAN-based business entity number, NNNN is the unique number, and A-Z-Z-Z is the check digit.
If you have recently registered for GST, make sure to use the new GST number and not the old VAT or service tax registration number.

question: What is your company's Permanent Account Number (PAN)?
helperText: The PAN is a unique 10-digit alphanumeric number issued by the Income Tax Department of India. It is mandatory for all businesses to have a valid PAN.
guidanceTips: The PAN number should be entered exactly as it appears on your company's registration documents.
Ensure that there are no typos or errors in the PAN number, as it is a critical identifier for your business.
If you do not have a PAN number yet, you will need to apply for one before proceeding with the insurance application.

question: What is the primary business activity or industry of your organization?
helperText: Select the option that best describes your primary business activity.
guidanceTitle: Guidance on selecting the right business type
guidanceTips: Choose the industry that accounts for the majority of your revenue and operations.
If your business activities span multiple industries, select the one that is most relevant to your public liability exposure.
If your business type is not listed, select 'Other' and provide a brief description in the next field.

question: What year was your business established?
helperText: Enter the 4-digit year your business was founded.
guidanceTips: The year of establishment is the year your business was officially registered or started operations.
Provide the exact 4-digit year, e.g., 2015, not a range like 2010-2015.
This information is important for the insurer to understand the maturity and experience of your business.

question: What is the total number of employees in your organization?
placeholder: Enter the exact number of employees
helperText: Please provide the exact number of employees in your organization. This information is used to calculate the appropriate insurance coverage and premium.
guidanceTitle: Guidance on Providing the Number of Employees
guidanceTips: The number of employees should include all full-time, part-time, and contractual workers in your organization.
Provide the current number of employees, not a range or estimate. This ensures accurate pricing and coverage for your policy.
If your employee count changes significantly during the policy period, you may need to update the information with your insurance provider.

question: What is your company's total annual revenue?
placeholder: Enter your annual revenue in INR
helperText: Please provide your company's total revenue for the last financial year. This information is required to calculate the appropriate public liability insurance premium.
guidanceTips: Enter the exact annual revenue figure, do not provide a range.
Ensure the revenue figure is for the most recent completed financial year.
If your company's financial year is different from the standard April-March, provide the revenue for the last 12-month period.
Revenue should include all income sources, not just from your core business activities.

question: What is the complete address of your business?
helperText: Provide the full address including street, city, state, and PIN code.
guidanceTips: Include the building number, street name, locality, city, state, and PIN code.
Ensure the address is accurate and matches your business registration documents like GST and PAN.
Avoid using abbreviations and provide the complete address details.

question: What is the city where your business is located?
helperText: Please enter the exact city name where your business is registered and operating from.
guidanceTips: Provide the full city name, not abbreviations or short forms.
Ensure the spelling is accurate as per official records.
If your business has multiple locations, enter the primary city of operation.
This information is important for determining the applicable state and local taxes (GST) for your insurance policy.

question: In which state is your business located?
options: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
helperText: Select the state where your business is located.
guidanceTips: Choose the state that matches the registered address of your business.
If your business has multiple locations, select the state where your primary operations are based.
Ensure that the state selected matches the information provided in your GST registration and other official documents.

question: What is the PIN code of the business location?
helperText: The 6-digit PIN code is required to determine the geographic location of your business for insurance coverage.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering the PIN Code
guidanceTips: The PIN code is a 6-digit number that uniquely identifies the geographic area where your business is located.
You can find the PIN code for your business address on any utility bill, government document, or by searching online.
Ensure you enter the correct 6-digit PIN code without any spaces or special characters.

question: What is the name of the primary contact person for this insurance policy?
placeholder: Enter full name of contact person
helperText: Provide the full legal name of the primary point of contact for this insurance policy.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering Contact Person Name
guidanceTips: Enter the full legal name as it appears on government-issued ID documents like Aadhaar or PAN card.
Avoid using initials or nicknames. Use the person's complete first and last name.
Make sure the spelling and capitalization match official records to ensure accurate policy documentation.

question: What is your business email address?
helperText: Please provide a valid business email address. This will be used for all communication regarding your insurance policy.
guidanceTips: Use your official business email address, not a personal email.
Ensure the email address is spelled correctly without any typos.
Avoid using generic email addresses like info@, contact@, etc. Use a specific individual's email if possible.

question: What is your business's primary contact phone number?
placeholder: Enter your 10-digit mobile or landline number
helperText: Please provide a valid 10-digit phone number where you can be reached.
guidanceTips: Enter your primary business contact number, which can be a mobile or landline.
The number should be 10 digits long and start with 6, 7, 8, or 9.
Ensure the number provided is active and regularly monitored, as the insurance provider may need to contact you regarding your policy.

question: When would you like the policy to start?
helperText: The policy period is typically 1 year. Please select a start date within the next 12 months.
guidanceTips: Choose a start date that aligns with your business operations and insurance needs.
The policy period is typically 1 year, so select a date that provides coverage for the full year.
Ensure the start date is not in the past and falls within the next 12 months.

question: When would you like the policy to end?
helperText: The policy period is typically 1 year. Please select the desired end date for your coverage.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Selecting the Policy End Date
guidanceTips: The policy end date should be exactly 1 year from the start date of the coverage.
Ensure the end date falls within the current Indian financial year (April 1st to March 31st).
Consider your business operations and any seasonal factors that may impact your insurance needs.

question: What is the total coverage amount required for your public liability insurance policy?
helperText: The coverage amount should be between ₹10 lakhs to ₹10 crores, as per industry standards for public liability insurance.
guidanceTips: Consider the nature and scale of your business operations, as well as the potential risks and liabilities you may face.
Review your past claims history, if any, to estimate the coverage needed.
Consult with your insurance advisor to determine the optimal coverage amount based on your specific business requirements.
Keep in mind that the coverage amount should be sufficient to protect your business from major liability claims and legal expenses.

question: Who is your current public liability insurance provider?
placeholder: Enter the name of your current insurance provider
helperText: Provide the name of the insurance company that currently provides your public liability coverage.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering Current Insurance Provider
guidanceTips: Enter the full legal name of the insurance company, not just the brand name.
If you have multiple public liability policies, provide the name of the primary insurance provider.
Ensure the information you provide matches your current insurance documents.

question: Please provide details of any previous claims made under public liability insurance policies in the last 5 years.
helperText: Provide a brief summary of any claims made, including the date, nature of the claim, amount claimed, and whether the claim was successful.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing Previous Claims History
guidanceTips: Be as detailed as possible in describing the previous claims, including the date, nature of the incident, amount claimed, and whether the claim was paid out.
Ensure that you provide accurate and truthful information, as this will be used to assess the risk profile of your business.
If you have not made any previous claims, simply state 'No previous claims' in the field.

question: Please describe the risk management measures in place at your business premises.
placeholder: Provide details on safety equipment, emergency procedures, employee training, etc.
helperText: Provide a detailed overview of the risk management practices followed at your business location.
guidanceTips: Describe the safety equipment installed, such as fire extinguishers, smoke detectors, and emergency exits.
Explain the emergency response procedures in place, including evacuation plans and employee training.
Mention any regular safety inspections, maintenance of equipment, and employee awareness programs implemented.
